---
title: "STA106_Midterm2_Project_RCode"
author: "Samuel Huang"
date: "2026-02-27"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra) 
library(car)      
library(EnvStats) 
library(emmeans)
```

# Load the data
```{r}
df <- read.csv("poisons.csv")

# poison and treatment are factors
df$poison <- factor(df$poison)
df$treat <- factor(df$treat)
```

\newpage

# Figures
```{r}
# Figure 1: Overall Histogram of Survival Time
 ggplot(df, aes(x = time)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "skyblue", color = "black", bins = 15) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Survival Times (Overall)",
       x = "Survival Time",
       y = "Frequency") 

# Figure 2: Boxplot by Poison Type
ggplot(df, aes(x = poison, y = time, fill = poison)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Survival Time by Poison Type",
       x = "Poison Type",
       y = "Survival Time")

# Figure 3: Boxplot by Treatment Type
ggplot(df, aes(x = treat, y = time, fill = treat)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Survival Time by Treatment Type",
       x = "Treatment Type",
       y = "Survival Time")

# Figure 4:survival Time by Treatment and Poison Type
ggplot(df, aes(x = treat, y = time, fill = poison)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Survival Time by Treatment and Poison Type",
       x = "Treatment Type",
       y = "Survival Time")
```

\newpage

# Summaries
```{r}
# 1. Grouped Summary (Poison & Treatment)
summary_stats <- df %>%
  group_by(poison, treat) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Summary by Poison Type
poison_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(poison) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Summary by Treatment Type
treat_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(treat) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4. Overall Summary  
overall_stats <- df %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Time = mean(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Time = sd(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )
```

\newpage

# Tables
```{r}

# Table 1: Overall 
knitr::kable(
  overall_stats,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Overall Summary Statistics",
  col.names = c("Overall Mean Time", "Overall Std. Deviation", "Total Sample Size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Table 2: Grouped
knitr::kable(
  summary_stats,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistics by Poison and Treatment",
  col.names = c("Poison", "Treatment", "Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample Size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Table 3: By Poison Type
knitr::kable(
  poison_summary,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistics by Poison Type",
  col.names = c("Poison Type", "Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample Size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# Table 4: By Treatment Type
knitr::kable(
  treat_summary,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Summary Statistics by Treatment Type",
  col.names = c("Treatment Type", "Mean Time", "Std. Deviation", "Sample Size")
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)
```

# Diagnosistic
```{r}
# fit initial model
model_initial <- lm(time ~ poison * treat, data = df)

# 1. Outlier Check with Studentized Residuals and Bonferroni cutoff
nt <- nrow(df)
a <- length(unique(df$poison))
b <- length(unique(df$treat))
alpha <- 0.05
t.cutoff <- qt(1 - alpha / (2 * nt), nt - a * b) 
rij <- rstandard(model_initial)
outliers <- which(abs(rij) > t.cutoff)
cat("Outliers detected at rows:", paste(outliers, collapse=", "), "\n")

# 2. Normality Check
car::qqPlot(model_initial$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Initial Model")
shapiro_initial <- shapiro.test(model_initial$residuals)
print(shapiro_initial)

# 3. Constant Variance Check
plot(model_initial, which = 3, main = "Resiual v.s. Fitted: Initial Model")
levene_initial <- leveneTest(model_initial)
print(levene_initial)

# 4. Evalutate Two Transformations
# Test Box-Cox Transformation
# get best lambda using Shapiro-Wilk
L2 <- boxcox(model_initial, objective.name = "Shapiro-Wilk", optimize = TRUE)$lambda

# apply transformation
df$time_box_trans <- (df$time^L2 - 1) / L2

# fit new model with transformed data
model_box_trans <- lm(time_box_trans ~ poison * treat, data = df)

# redo diagnostics on transformed model
car::qqPlot(model_box_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Box-cox Transformed Model")
print(shapiro.test(model_box_trans$residuals))
plot(model_box_trans, which = 3, main = "Resiual v.s. Fitted: Box-cox Transformed Model")
print(leveneTest(model_box_trans))


# Test Log Transformation
L3 <- boxcox(df$time, objective.name = "Log-Likelihood", optimize = TRUE)$lambda
df$time_log_trans <- (df$time^L3 - 1) / L3
model_log_trans <- lm(time_log_trans ~ poison * treat, data = df)

car::qqPlot(model_log_trans$residuals, id = FALSE, main = "QQ Plot: Log Transformed Model")
print(shapiro.test(model_log_trans$residuals))
plot(model_log_trans, which = 3, main = "Resiual v.s. Fitted: Log Transformed Model")
print(leveneTest(model_log_trans))


# select final model based on diagnostics
final_model <- model_log_trans
```

# Analysis
```{r}
# 1. ANOVA Table (Testing for Interactions and Main Effects)
anova_results <- anova(final_model)
knitr::kable(
  anova_results,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Two-Way ANOVA Table for Final Model"
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)

# 2. Confidence Intervals (4 Pairwise, 2 Contrasts)
# use estimated marginal means for the interaction
emm <- emmeans(final_model, ~ poison * treat)

# --- 4 Pairwise Comparisons ---
# compare treatments within each Poison level (w/ adjusted with Tukey)
pairwise_results <- pairs(emmeans(final_model, ~ treat | poison), adjust = "tukey")
pairwise_df <- as.data.frame(pairwise_results)
knitr::kable(
  pairwise_df,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Pairwise Comparisons of Treatments within Poison Types (Tukey Adjusted)"
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"))


# --- 2 Contrasts ---
# contrast 1: Poison 1 vs average of Poison 2 and 3 (across all treatments)
# contrast 2: Treatment A vs Treatment B (across all poisons)
emm_poison <- emmeans(final_model, ~ poison)
emm_treat <- emmeans(final_model, ~ treat)

contrast_list_poison <- list("Poison 1 vs (2+3)/2" = c(1, -0.5, -0.5))
contrast_list_treat <- list("Treat A vs Treat B" = c(1, -1, 0, 0))

contrast_poison_df <- as.data.frame(contrast(emm_poison, contrast_list_poison, adjust = "bonferroni"))
contrast_treat_df <- as.data.frame(contrast(emm_treat, contrast_list_treat, adjust = "bonferroni"))
combined_contrasts <- rbind(contrast_poison_df, contrast_treat_df)

knitr::kable(
  combined_contrasts,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Custom Contrasts (Bonferroni Adjusted)"
) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position", full_width = FALSE)
```
